meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Students: Solve Deployment Issues with a SaaS Starter Kit
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this SaaS starter kit guide for students to launch your first project using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure is a rite of passage, not a dead end. By adopting a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you abstract away infrastructure complexity. Leverage tools like Antigravity for automated boilerplate and Claude Code for real-time debugging to move from local development to a live production URL in minutes.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks hunched over your keyboard, refining the perfect logic, only to hit the 'Deployment Wall.' The console logs are screaming, your environment variables are out of sync, and the dream of a live, polished SaaS app feels like it is slipping away. You aren't failing—you are just missing the bridge between code and cloud.

Professional engineers rarely build from scratch. They use battle-tested starter kits that handle the heavy lifting of authentication, database schema, and CI/CD pipelines. This guide will transform your workflow, turning deployment from a source of anxiety into a seamless, automated milestone in your engineering journey.

steps.title: Initialize with a SaaS Starter Kit
steps.description: Select a boilerplate that includes pre-configured Auth and Database connections to avoid common setup errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS starter kit with Next.js, Supabase auth, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the structure includes a dedicated /lib folder for database utility functions.

steps.title: Configure Environment Variables
steps.description: Centralize your secrets to ensure your code remains portable and secure across environments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .env.example file listing all required keys for Supabase, Stripe, and Resend. Add a validation script to ensure no keys are missing at runtime.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Use automated migration scripts to keep your development and production schemas perfectly in sync.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a migration script for a 'User' profile table with fields for subscription_status and credit_balance.

steps.title: Implement CI/CD Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repository to a cloud provider so every push to 'main' triggers an automatic deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Vercel-compatible yaml configuration for automated testing and deployment on every merge to the main branch.

steps.title: Smoke Test in Production
steps.description: Verify your live endpoints to ensure your app is accessible to the world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a health-check script that pings the /api/health endpoint and logs the deployment latency.

pro_tips: Always use a 'staging' environment before pushing to production; it is the single best habit for avoiding public bugs.
Debug with intent: Instead of guessing errors, ask Claude Code to 'explain the stack trace' to identify the root cause in milliseconds.
Keep your database schema versioned in git; if your local DB drifts from your production DB, your app will inevitably crash.
Use Feature Flags to push code to production without exposing unfinished features to your users.
Optimize your build size early; large bundles are the leading cause of 'deployment timeout' errors on hobby-tier hosting plans.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, focus on gathering user feedback. Use the analytics tools provided by your starter kit to track conversion and iterate based on real-world usage data.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the guide, and the roadmap to ship your first production-grade application. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' code and start shipping the code you have. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
